title: Remote Desktop and Screen-Sharing Setup
text:
Q: How do I set up remote desktop and screen-sharing on my computer?
A: Windows: Step 1: Open the Start menu and type 'Remote Desktop Connection' to find the app. Step 2: Click on 'Show Options' and enter the computer name or IP address of the remote machine. Step 3: Click 'Connect' and enter your credentials when prompted. Step 4: Ensure that the remote computer has 'Allow remote connections to this computer' enabled in System Properties under the Remote tab. macOS: Step 1: Download and install the 'Microsoft Remote Desktop' app from the App Store. Step 2: Open the app and click on the '+' icon to add a new connection. Step 3: Enter the PC name or IP address and your user credentials. Step 4: Make sure that the remote Mac has 'Remote Management' enabled in System Preferences under Sharing. For screen-sharing, Windows users can use 'Quick Assist' by searching for it in the Start menu, while Mac users can enable 'Screen Sharing' in the Sharing preferences. Always ensure that both computers are on the same network or have proper VPN access for remote connections.
